text: Fill in the blanks on the anatomy of the nasal cavity and paranasal sinuses
questions: The nasal septum is formed by the septal cartilage anteriorly, the perpendicular plate of the *ethmoid* bone superiorly, and the vomer inferiorly.
Little's area (Kiesselbach's plexus) is the site of *90/ninety* percent of all epistaxis cases.
The *ostiomeatal* complex is the key functional unit through which the maxillary, frontal, and anterior ethmoidal sinuses drain into the middle meatus.
The nasolacrimal duct drains into the *inferior* meatus of the nasal cavity.
The posterior ethmoidal sinuses drain into the *superior* meatus.
The *sphenopalatine* artery is the largest artery supplying the nasal cavity and is a branch of the maxillary artery.
The sphenoidal sinus drains into the *sphenoethmoidal* recess, located above the superior turbinate.
Nerve supply of the nasal cavity comes from CN *V1* (anterior/superior) and CN V2 (posterior/inferior).
Deviated nasal septum most commonly occurs at the junction of the *vomer* and the septal cartilage.
Functional Endoscopic Sinus Surgery (FESS) is the primary surgical treatment for chronic *sinusitis* that does not respond to medical management.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the material and try again.
